labels.notes: Irene Martel-Jeddry 11:31am — cervicogenic headache, polypharmacy, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED OCCIPITAL/CERVICAL NUMBING INJECTION (real procedure). DEFECTS: (1) procedure section had transcript-quote bracket leak (visual SOAP clutter — should be hidden from rendered SOAP, kept only in JSON for billing audit); (2) DIAGNOSTIC CODE 491 (Chronic bronchitis) is wildly wrong — patient has no respiratory complaint. Expected dx 729 (Fibrositis/myositis/muscular rheumatism) or 723/725 (cervicalgia/polymyalgia rheumatica) — neck/cervical pain. tools-model returned semantically-untethered code; needs DB-description-vs-primary-diagnosis cross-check guard. (3) BILLING MISSED INJECTION: only A007A + Q310A. Expected G231A (peripheral nerve block) for the occipital injection. clinical_features extraction missed nerve_block_peripheral despite SOAP procedure section explicitly listing 'performed ultrasound-guided cervical numbing injection'. (4) Dropdown for occipital nerve block did not surface G291/G264 — those codes are not in our 235-code DB; G231A is the SOB-current code per the v0.10.55 audit. Either the user's expected codes are from a different SOB version or our DB needs an audit. SEPARATE ISSUE: drug-name STT/LLM hallucinations (lemicil, Razipan, cimetidine-as-acetaminophen) — needs verbatim-only rule for unrecognized drug names.
